## 結論先講

方案 B **技術上可落地、改動量比提案估的大一點**,但提案的**價值定位有兩個事實偏差**,落地前需要先校準,否則會做出「只覆蓋一半路徑、又跟既有機制重疊」的東西。

---

## 一、提案假設 vs 實際碼(事實核對)

| 提案假設 | 實際 | 判定 |
|---|---|---|
| `tool-loop.ts` / `runToolLoop` / `ToolLoopOptions` / `executeToolCalls` 存在 | ✅ 全部存在(tool-loop.ts:61/77/199) | 對齊 |
| 執行點在「results append 到 messages 之後」 | ✅ tool-loop.ts:137-143 確實是 append 迴圈 | 對齊 |
| hook ctx 可拿 `r.name / r.args / r.isError / r.output` | ❌ `ToolResult` 只有 `{id, output}`(tool-loop.ts:194-197) | **不對齊** |
| 把「Tool usage logging」遷移成 `*` hook | ❌ logging 不在 tool-loop.ts,而在 `sessionManager.ts:387/394` 的 `logEvent("tool_call",...)`,且 DirectProvider 已透過 `onToolEnd`→`emitUpdate("tool_call_update")` 走同一條(direct.ts:136-139) | **已存在,非待遷移** |

---

## 二、最關鍵的可行性問題:覆蓋範圍只有一半

專案有**兩條 tool 執行路徑**:

1. **DirectProvider**(本地 LLM / 直連 API)→ `runToolLoop`(in-process,OpenAI-format tool call,bridge 能拿到 input/output/result 物件)
2. **ACP adapter**(Kiro / Codex / Claude — 依 CLAUDE.md 是**目前主力路徑**)→ agent **進程外自跑 tool loop**,bridge 只透過 session update 看到 `tool_call` / `tool_call_update` 事件(sessionManager.ts:373),**拿不到 tool 的真實 args/output,也無法 mutate**

方案 B 把 hook 加在 `tool-loop.ts` → **只對 DirectProvider 生效**。內建 hooks 表裡那幾項(CodeGraph enrichment、AI.md hint)本來就只在 DirectProvider 跑,對齊;但若期待「post-tool hook 統一管所有 tool 行為」,ACP 路徑根本進不來。這是結構性限制,不是改幾行能補的。

---

## 三、跟既有擴充點重疊(方案 B 的邊際價值被高估)

`tool-loop.ts:70` **已有 `onToolEnd?: (name, result, isError) => void`** 這個 per-tool callback。

- **純觀察類**(logging / notification / fail tracking)→ `onToolEnd` + sessionManager 的 `logEvent` **已經能做**,不需要方案 B。提案方案 A 的「tool usage log」其實已被近期 commit 實作掉了。
- 方案 B 相對 `onToolEnd` 的**真正增量** = ① 能 **mutate `messages` / output**(注入 hint、改寫結果)② matcher-based 註冊。`onToolEnd` 只能觀察不能改。

所以方案 B 值不值得做,取決於你**是否真的需要「改寫 tool 結果 / 注入 message」**。如果只是要記錄與分析,殺雞用牛刀。

---

## 四、若仍要落地,真實改動清單(比提案 ~80 行多)

1. **加 `matchGlob`**(~15 行)— 提案有估到
2. **加 `PostToolHook` interface + `ToolLoopOptions.postToolHooks`**(~10 行)
3. **加 hook 執行迴圈**(~10 行)
4. ⚠️ **擴 `ToolResult` 介面**:現在是 `{id, output}`,要補 `name / args / isError`(executeToolCalls 內部有這些值但 return 時丟掉了 — tool-loop.ts:240/244)。否則提案的 `r.name/r.args/r.isError` 編譯不過。連帶要改 executeToolCalls 的 return shape(~10 行,且要看有無其他消費者)
5. ⚠️ **CodeGraph enrichment 遷移有語意陷阱**:它現在跑在 `executeToolCalls` **內部、serialize+truncate 之前**(tool-loop.ts:228-232),mutate 的是結構化 `result` 物件;搬成「append 後的 post hook」會變成對**已截斷的字串** output 操作,**行為會變**。這不是 drop-in,要嘛保留原位、要嘛重寫 enrichment 讓它吃字串。
6. AI.md / CodeGraph hint(tool-loop.ts:145-165)→ 這項可乾淨遷移成 `*` hook ✅

實際約 **100–120 行 + 1 個語意風險點**,而非提案的 80 行。

---

## 建議(push back)

⚠️ **我建議先別直接照方案 B 實作**

- **理由**:① 覆蓋只有 DirectProvider 一半路徑,跟「通用 post-tool 擴充點」的賣點不符;② 純觀察需求已被 `onToolEnd` + `logEvent` 覆蓋,方案 B 的增量只在「mutation」;③ CodeGraph enrichment 遷移有 truncation 語意漂移風險。
- **替代方案**:先確認你的**實際需求是「觀察」還是「改寫」**——
  - 只要觀察/分析(/dream、熱力圖)→ 既有機制夠用,不需方案 B。
  - 需要對 DirectProvider 注入 hint / 改寫結果 → 做**精簡版方案 B**:只加 hook registry + matcher,**把 CodeGraph enrichment 留在原位不遷移**(避開語意陷阱),只把「AI.md hint」這類 append 後邏輯遷成 hook。
- **若仍要照原提案全做**:請說「OK 用原方案」,我會把 ToolResult 擴展與 enrichment 重寫一併納入設計。

要我針對「精簡版方案 B」出一份含因果鏈分析的設計規格(走 `/dev-design`),還是你先決定需求是觀察還是改寫?

<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

<title>Plan C (Instinct Observer) architecture for automated skill extraction from tool usage</title>
<subtitle>Defines learning loop that analyzes tool patterns via local LLM to auto-generate skill proposals</subtitle>
<facts>

<fact>Local LLM analysis triggers every N tool calls to cluster observations and update instincts.json</fact>
<fact>Auto skill proposal occurs when confidence ≥ 0.5 and occurrences ≥ 5 via SKILL_PROPOSE mechanism</fact>
<fact>New /dream step "instinct-evolve" scans observations.jsonl and runs local LLM clustering</fact>
<fact>Implementation estimated at ~300 lines plus new tool-observer.ts file</fact>
<fact>Recommended path is A→B→C progressive validation: A proves tool logging value, B prepares architecture, C adds learning loop</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Plan C (Instinct Observer) defines a self-learning closed loop for automatic skill extraction. The system records tool execution metadata to observations.jsonl, capturing not just individual tool calls but also preceding tool sequences to detect combo patterns. A local LLM periodically analyzes these observations (triggered every N calls) to cluster usage patterns into "instincts" — reusable patterns with confidence scores. When an instinct reaches confidence ≥ 0.5 and occurs ≥ 5 times, it automatically triggers SKILL_PROPOSE. The architecture includes fallback to pure statistical analysis when local LLM is unavailable. The proposal recommends incremental rollout: Plan A first validates that tool logging provides analytical value, Plan B prepares the hook architecture, then Plan C adds the learning loop without modifying core code.</narrative>

<subtitle>skill-suggest.ts scans recent sessions for recurring bigrams without matching skills to propose candidates</subtitle>

<fact>skill-suggest.ts implements frequency-based skill suggestion by scanning sessions/*.md files from last 7 days</fact>
<fact>suggestSkills() extracts user messages, tokenizes into bigrams, and counts occurrences across sessions</fact>

<narrative>The existing skill suggestion system provides pattern detection infrastructure that Plan C could leverage. skill-suggest.ts implements a frequency-based approach that scans recent session markdown files (7-day window), extracts user messages, tokenizes them into bigrams and meaningful single words, then counts cross-session occurrences. Candidates must appear in at least 3 different sessions and not match existing skill keywords to avoid duplication. The system returns the top 10 patterns with their occurrence counts and example messages. This establishes the baseline: current skill suggestions come from user message analysis. Plan C would extend this by analyzing tool usage patterns instead of (or in addition to) user message patterns, feeding tool-derived instincts into the same SKILL_PROPOSE mechanism once confidence and occurrence thresholds are met.</narrative>
